proof_sketch: The mutant changes the boundary condition from `idx < size` to `idx < size - 1` only in the half-precision kernel specialization (`template<> __global__ void gelu_kernel<half>`). The input specification fixes dtype to torch.float32, so the dispatch macro `AT_DISPATCH_FLOATING_TYPES_AND_HALF` selects the generic template (unchanged) for all test inputs. Hence the mutated code path is never executed, and the outputs are bitwise identical regardless of the values.

This would ensure that kernels with per-type specializations are fully validated."
